segments.text: OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA
Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 3, Chapter No. 18, Text No. 6
SATURBYAMANU ARIDURUKKATOMARAYA
TAMSTRAGRAH GAMGAM APALAKSHABHITAM
TODAMRISHAN NIRGAD AMBUMADYAD
RAHATASAKARENUR YATHEBHA
RAHATASAKARENUR YATHEBHA
Translation and purport by His Divine Eminence,
A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada
Translation
Although the Lord was pained by the sharp-like abusive words of the demons,
He bore the pain.
But seeing that the earth on the ends of His tusk was frightened,
He rose out of the water, just as an elephant emerges with its female companion
when assailed by an alligator.
Purport
The Mayavadi philosophers cannot understand that the Lord has feelings.
The Lord is satisfied.
The Lord is satisfied if someone offers Him a nice prayer.
And similarly, if someone decries His existence or calls Him by ill names,
God is dissatisfied.
The Supreme Personality of Godhead is decried by the Mayavadi philosophers who are almost demons.
They say that God has no head, no form, no existence, and no legs, hands, or other bodily limbs.
In other words, they say that He is dead or lame.
All this is not true.
All these misconceptions of the Supreme Lord are a source of dissatisfaction to Him.
He is never pleased with such atheistic descriptions.
In this case, although the Lord felt sorrow from the piercing words of the demons,
He delivered the earth for the satisfaction of the demigods, who are ever His devotees.
The conclusion is that God is as sentient as we are.
He is satisfied by our prayers and dissatisfied by our harsh words against Him.
In order to give protection to His devotees, He is always ready to tolerate insulting words from the Atheists.
Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta purport.
So this chapter, the battle between Lord Bohr and Hiranaksha.
So in the previous verses, we have read how Hiranaksha used very abusive words.
And in this verse, it's mentioned, the Lord was pained by the shaft-like abusive words of the demon.
But he bore the pain.
So Prabhupada in the purport mentions, the Lord also has feelings.
He is a sentient being.
Just like we have feelings, we are sentient beings, we are amsa of the Lord.
We being amsa of the Lord, the Lord Himself, who is Purna, who is the source of all these amsas,
or who is the complete whole, the absolute truth, He is also a person, a sentient being.
And Prabhupada mentions, because he has feelings,
bhavag rahayu janardana.
When someone offers prayers, the Lord is very pleased.
We being amsa, we also have the tendency, if someone calls us by our name,
or someone praises us,
or glorifies us, we also have the tendency, if someone calls us by our name, or someone praises us,
or glorifies us, we also feel some pleasure.
Although that's not our constitutional position, to be glorified.
However, we are the predominators, and our constitutional position is to glorify the predominator.
So just like, to give a crude example, we also experience pain and pleasure. If someone praises us, we feel pleasure. If someone criticizes us, passes a sarcastic remark, we feel pain.
So here, the demon used abusive words and it pained the Lord. All said and done, the demon also is amsha of the Lord.
And the Lord is said to be Shad Aishwarya Purnam. He has unlimited strength, unlimited wealth, unlimited knowledge, unlimited fame, unlimited beauty,
unlimited renunciation. And other good qualities, which we see, is also there. The Lord is embodiment of such good qualities.
Titiksva Karunikaha. Titiksva means tolerant. So Lord is also very tolerant. Sometimes we wonder, just two days back somebody asked me this question.
Very sarcastically, where was Lord when the Muslims invaded and ransacked the temples, destroyed the temples, destroyed the deities?
Actually, it can be very bewildering. Unless we have proper shravanam and we have understood,
so this person who was asking me appeared to be quite, he was not an atheist, but from his question it was quite evident that he is bewildered.
Where was the Lord? Sometimes it becomes difficult to reconcile.
If Lord is all-powerful, he is the supreme controller, not a blade of grass moves without his sanction, then how do we reconcile all these things happening in the world?
So the Lord is very, very tolerant.
The jivas, who are his amshas, can say,
the jivas, who are his amshas, can sometimes decry the Lord, can abuse the Lord, can become envious of him, can blaspheme the Lord.
He has given the free will. Free will means we have a choice to do whatever we want.
We can decry the Lord, we can blaspheme the Lord, we can glorify the Lord, we can pray to the Lord, we can abuse the Lord.
He tolerates.
On one side we see temples were ransacked, deities were, so to say, broken, the Lord tolerated.
No doubt in this world every action has a reaction.
Newton also pointed out this, every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
When we see that we wonder why the Lord is not, is the Lord really there?
The Lord tolerates.
And other side when we see some calamities, when we see some people suffering very miserably, again we get bewildered.
Why the Lord is so cruel? Why the Lord is so unkind?
The fact is we all are getting what is due to us.
We can abuse the Lord, the Lord has given us a free will, but we will get back the reaction for our deeds.
So here, as I mentioned, Lord Shad Aishwarya Purnam, he has many, many good qualities, an aboundment of all such good qualities.
One of the qualities he is abounding here is he bore the insult.
Because he has given us a free will, we can abuse the Lord, we can blaspheme the Lord.
There are great atheists in this world,
who very strongly put forth arguments, God does not exist.
He gives the intelligence.
Even we as devotees sometimes we may act in ways, although in our words we can say that yes, we are devotees of Krishna,
we surrender to Krishna, we depend on Krishna.
But sometimes our actions can prove otherwise.
Actions speak louder than words.
The Lord is there in the heart, he is witness to all our actions, all our feelings.
Sometimes we may have an element of distrust or faithlessness.
Sometimes when devotees also go through some rough patches or challenges in life,
our faith in the Lord can shake.
One time there was a devotee, Visala Das, he was foreign-bodied, he was from America.
He had come to India and he was going through lot of challenges in his life.
So he came to Vrindavan and because of all the ups and downs in his life,
even his Krishna consciousness was getting affected.
His chanting, his sadhana.
So he, just like we go through challenges, so we try to find various remedies.
We go to the doctor, we go to somebody.
So he went to an astrologer.
And the astrologer pointed out that there is an influence of the stars, Rahu, Rahu planet.
And because of that, he is going through this challenge.
And to counteract the influence of Rahu planets, he should wear a yantra.
So as advised by the astrologer, he bought the yantra, he wore it.
Quite a big one and it was quite visible.
He wore it on his neck.
So he was quite satisfied, okay, now at least.
And his whole objective was that how somehow he could counter all the evil influences
which is there in his life and then he can peacefully chant Hare Krishna,
peacefully practice Krishna consciousness.
So he came to the temple just as he was entering the temple, Krishna Balram temple.
Another very senior disciple of Prabhupada.
He stopped him, he said, where are you coming from?
And this yantra what he was wearing on the neck was quite visible.
So he said, what is this?
So he told, I had gone to the astrologer and he has given me this yantra
and he has told that it will counter all the influence of the Rahu planet.
So then he immediately stopped him there and said,
but Prabhupada has never asked us to go to astrologer.
Prabhupada said we should have faith in Krishna.
Sarva dharman parityajya maam ekam sharanam raja aham tam sarva paap eva.
He chanted that shloka.
Krishna will counter all the evil influences, we have to just surrender to him.
Krishna is saying maam ekam.
Why are you going to an astrologer?
So he said, why don't you go and meet Prabhupada?
In the meantime this devotee went and told Prabhupada,
Prabhupada this is what has happened, this devotee has gone to an astrologer
and he has got this yantra, he is wearing that yantra to counter the influences of Rahu planet.
So this devotee went and met Shloka Prabhupada.
So Prabhupada saw him, he said where had you gone?
So he told him, I had gone, who is he?
How much did you pay him?
So Prabhupada was quite unhappy.
And then he said, Shloka Prabhupada quoted the verse,
Sarva dharman parityajya,
He said the whole Shloka 18.66 and Prabhupada said,
If you just surrender to Krishna, Krishna will with a slight kick,
annihilate one lakh Rahu planets.
If you just surrender to Krishna, Krishna with a slight kick, not full kick,
slight kick can annihilate one lakh Rahu planets.
So even we sometimes, again we are conditioned souls,
faith is also, only a pure devotee has complete dependence on the Lord.
We have examples of great devotees like Prahalad, he was thrown in a pit of snakes,
imagine hissing snake, even one snake if we see from a distance we get fearful.
So we may not have that faith and our actions prove it,
our actions prove that.
The Lord is there in our heart, he is witness to all our feelings, all our actions
and sometimes the Lord is pained.
We may chant, but then our actions prove otherwise.
We may not have dependence on the Lord, we may not have faith in his protection.
The Lord tolerates.
Because the Lord is very tolerant, we are all continuing in Krishna consciousness.
Otherwise even as devotees we end up doing so many nonsense things,
which is very painful to the Lord, painful to the spiritual master.
They are very tolerant, they are very forgiving, very kind, very merciful.
Again we fall down, again they give us an opportunity.
So long as we somehow, because just like a child when he is learning to walk,
he cannot just run all of a sudden, he will fall down.
And many times when we also make mistakes, we fall down,
one thing is yes, we should feel there is an element of guilt,
but beyond a point we shouldn't lament.
Learn from the mistake, the Lord is very tolerant.
Sometimes people also say, I have done so many sinful activities in the past,
what is the hope for me?
The Lord is very forgiving.
We try our best not to repeat those mistakes,
we try our best to not commit sinful activities intentionally.
Unintentionally by the force of kala, guna, karma,
sometimes mistakes happen, we move on.
We beg forgiveness, we offer our prayers.
We beg for strength and move on.
So here, just like Prabhupada, the devotee was telling,
we are following a process.
Adho, Shraddha, Tata, Sadhu, Sangha, Bhajan, Kriya, Anartha,
all the misgivings, all the doubts, all the faithlessness what we have.
Just like Prabhupada is saying, mayavadi fila,
they don't even have faith in the Lord, the Lord tolerates.
Then a stage comes, nishtha, firm faith.
Then we don't doubt.
Then we have strong conviction in the presence of the Lord,
in the protection of the Lord.
We cannot artificially imitate that stage.
I gave example of Prahlad Maharaj.
There is a very nice conversation in
Perfect Question Perfect Answer book where
one devotee asked Prabhupada,
Prabhupada, many times we come across prasadam which is spoiled.
You know, sometimes prasadam which is left over for many days,
you may see some fungus or you may say something.
So he asked Prabhupada, can we honor that prasadam,
can we take that prasadam?
So Prabhupada says, for a pure devotee, it does not matter, he will honor it.
Even if it is apparently spoiled fungus.
Because he is not in bodily concept.
He is completely at the spiritual platform, transcendental.
When he takes prasadam also, he is just absorbing the transcendence.
He is unaffected.
But he says, neophyte devotees or devotees like us
should not imitate pure devotees.
If the prasadam is spoiled, we cannot imitate pure devotees.
Okay, let me also eat, I will not be affected.
So, Prabhupada many times talked about imitation
and following in the footsteps of pure devotees.
There is a difference between the two.
One time in Vrindavan, devotees, somehow the word started spreading
that if a person
passes stool one time in a day, he is a yogi.
If he passes stool two times, he is a bhogi.
And more than two times, he is a rogi.
So sometimes devotees also, they want to show off, there is some kind of a competition.
So some devotees would hold on.
And somehow the news reached Prabhupada.
And one devotee goes and says,
some devotees, even though they may have stomach ache,
but somehow they hold on because they want to prove that they are yogis.
So when Prabhupada heard that,
said, is it? Devotees are doing it?
Acha?
He was quite surprised.
Conditioned souls, all the envy, jealousy, want to prove a point,
want to show that they are great devotees.
Pure devotee of the Lord has none of these agendas.
Someone praises, it's fine.
Someone blasphemes, it's fine.
Someone, you know, because they are absorbed in the Lord.
We conditioned souls are our satchidananda, we project in various things.
So, so Prabhupada said, Acha?
And then, he told a very humorous story.
To demonstrate that how,
devotees sometimes imitate.
He said there was one time,
a clerk in the office, he was assigned by his supervisor,
the task of copying, making fair notes.
There was a rough book.
Sometimes in earlier times, unlike present times, you have soft copies,
it's all in the computer, you can take a backup.
There could be some gazettes or some kind of official papers
and after 20 years, 25 years, 30 years, you make a fresh copy.
You write it again.
So he was assigned the task of making a fair copy of something which was
there already in a book which was now worn out.
So while he was copying, suddenly he got up from his seat
and he was jumping all around the room trying to catch something.
So the boss came in and he is wondering what's happening to him.
He is supposed to be sitting in his seat and writing,
suddenly jumping and trying to catch something.
So he asked him, what are you up to?
He said, I am trying to catch a fly.
He said, why?
He said, come.
See in this book here, in this original book there is a fly
which is smashed and pasted here.
It could have happened 30 years back, 40 years back,
somebody was writing and the book was folded, fly came in and it got smashed.
And the fly was there in the book and he wants to fail that fly
by copying what was there already.
Without understanding the intent, if you blindly follow,
that's why bhakti is not just sentimental stuff.
Bhaktivedanta, it should be backed with proper understanding, proper knowledge.
Surrender also is not a sentimental thing.
Many times people are at a very sentimental level.
So bhakti has to be backed with proper understanding.
The faith in the Lord is not at a very sentimental level.
It has to be backed with tapasya, backed with purification, backed with anartha-livriti, backed with proper knowledge.
And when we are backed with proper knowledge, then we will not imitate.
On one side, yes, we have faith in the Lord.
Other side, whatever needs to be done as a matter of duty will be done.
Even though a devotee may take some preventive measures,
apparently may appear to be taking some protective steps,
still, he has complete faith in the protection of the Lord.
He has the faith that although I may take so many steps,
ultimately if the Lord sanctions, I will be protected.
Otherwise, you may have best of doctors, you may have best of medicines,
but if Krishna does not protect, the patient will die.
So here Prabhupada also mentions in the purport that the Lord,
irrespective of the words spoken by the boar,
seeing that mother earth was frightened because mother earth was inundated,
seeing the earth on the end of his tusk was frightened,
he rose out of the water, just an elephant emerges with its female companion when assailed by an alligator.
So then Prabhupada says,
in order to give protection to his devotee,
he is always ready to tolerate inserting words from the earthies.
Even when the Lord took, Lord Krishna took the vow not to fight in the battle,
not to take up any weapons,
the Lord broke his promise.
Generally a gentleman is supposed to hold on to his promise,
is to keep up his words.
That's the quality of a gentleman.
Once he has said, he doesn't go back on his words,
he has made a commitment.
The Lord made a commitment.
But for his devotee,
he broke that commitment.
Even at the cost of some people saying,
what kind of a Lord he is?
He is lying, he is stealing,
he is running away from the battle.
But if we understand from,
in a disciplic succession we understand,
it's all for protection of his devotees,
for giving pleasure to his devotees.
Even at the cost of his reputation,
even at the cost of people using abusive words.
One time there was
a person,
he was in an aircraft, he was flying,
and he got late somehow,
rushed into the aircraft,
reached the seat where he was supposed to sit,
put the luggage on the top,
and sat.
He was in the middle seat.
The window seat he saw some middle aged lady,
and on his left side, the aisle seat,
he saw a young girl,
seven years old girl.
Sitting all by herself, all alone.
The plane took off,
and she started speaking to that girl.
And he saw that this girl was
quite blissful, busy,
with her coloring book,
crayons were there,
and she was listening.
And he could see that,
you know, you can make out,
on the next seat, somebody else was sitting,
and on the front, back.
The girl was apparently all alone,
so he was wondering,
how is it this young girl, seven years old,
traveling all by herself?
Because on the right side,
somebody else was sitting,
front, back, left, right,
everyone, strangers.
And the girl all by herself is traveling,
so he was wondering,
how is it traveling alone?
And she was quite busy with,
you know, her coloring book,
and crayons, and all that.
And suddenly,
there was some turbulence in the plane.
The pilot came on the PA system,
and said,
we're going through rough weather,
and suddenly the plane started,
you know, wobbling.
Sudden dip happened,
and started, you know,
some unusual movement,
which was not usual for all the passengers.
And many of them got frightened,
some of them started screaming,
some of them started crying.
The lady on the right side,
sitting next to the window,
you know,
hands folded, started praying.
And this person also,
held onto the seat,
and, you know,
total commotion in the plane.
It was dipping,
and suddenly turning,
and turn happened, and...
And while all this thing was happening,
the girl, the small little girl,
was just sitting, playing with
the coloring book and crayons.
Totally unperturbed with what's happening.
So then, after about 20 minutes,
again the pilot came on the PA system,
and says,
I'm very, very sorry for all the
inconvenience caused.
We had gone through some rough weather.
Now it's all fine,
and very soon we'll be landing
the airport.
So everyone had a sigh of relief,
and they were quiet, you know,
took a deep breath,
and trying to relax.
And this person turned to the girl,
that young little girl,
and said,
tell me little one,
while all of us were,
all so-called adults,
were quite panic-stricken,
and we were all screaming,
you were completely...
blissful, happy, undisturbed.
By the way, why is it so?
Said, my father is the pilot.
He's in the cockpit.
He's taking me back home.
Why should I worry?
So the small little girl had the faith
that my father is there,
and my father is driving the plane.
He will help me reach my house safely.
So even for a devotee,
again, not imitation,
nishtha,
a stage comes
when we may go through some rough times in our life,
but we have firm faith
the Lord is in control.
It's easy to say,
it's easy to preach,
but difficult to practice.
A pure devotee of Lord
has that conviction.
Just like that little girl
had a strong conviction,
my father is there,
she has nothing to worry.
He will take him home.
Likewise, a devotee of the Lord,
he fearlessly practices Krishna Consciousness,
having firm faith
that the Lord,
my loving Father,
Supreme Lord,
is in charge of my life.
He will take care.
He'll take me back home,
back to Godhead.
So with these words,
we'll stop here.
Grantharaj,
Srimad Bhagavatam ki
Srila Prabhupada ki,
Satsang with Mooji
